hook: AI gave you a 7-step framework when you needed a one-line answer. Again.
caption:
AI gave you a 7-step framework when you needed a one-line answer. Again.

Here's what's actually happening:

ChatGPT doesn't know if you're a "give me the full picture" person or a "bottom line first, details on request" person.

So it guesses. And it usually guesses wrong for you specifically.

The Synthesizer type? Wants the conclusion up front, then the reasoning. Give them a numbered list first and they're already annoyed.

The Architect type? Needs the structure before the answer makes sense. Skip the framework and they feel like something's missing.

Same question. Completely different ideal response.

This is why "just be more specific in your prompts" advice only gets you halfway there. You shouldn't have to re-explain how your brain works every single conversation.

Your BootFile stores it. So AI already knows you want the short version first. Or the full context. Or bullet points. Or none of the above.

One profile. Every conversation calibrated to how you actually think.

Which one are you — bottom line first, or walk me through it?
